channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/obra — An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works.
item.description: Sponsors/obra is an agentic skills framework and software development methodology.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] influxdata/telegraf — Agent for collecting, processing, aggregating, and writing metrics, logs, and other arbitrary data.
item.description: Telegraf is a data collection and processing agent for metrics, logs, and arbitrary data aggregation.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills — A set of ready to use Agent Skills for research, science, engineering, analysis, finance and writing.
item.description: K-Dense-AI publishes scientific-agent-skills, pre-built AI capabilities for research, science, engineering, analysis, finance, and writing.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/danielmiessler — Agentic AI Infrastructure for magnifying HUMAN capabilities.
item.description: Agentic AI infrastructure project designed to augment and magnify human capabilities.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] ton-blockchain/acton — Toolchain for TON smart contract development and beyond
item.description: TON blockchain's Acton toolchain enables smart contract development and beyond.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/trycua — Open-source infrastructure for Computer-Use Agents. Sandboxes, SDKs, and benchmarks to train and evaluate AI agents that can control full desktops (macOS, Linux, Windows).
item.description: trycua provides open-source infrastructure with sandboxes, SDKs, and benchmarks for training and evaluating desktop-controlling AI agents.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Interpretable EEG Microstate Discovery via Variational Deep Embedding: A Systematic Architecture Search with Multi-Quadrant Evaluation
item.description: Researchers develop Conv-VaDE model for EEG microstate discovery, improving interpretability through variational embedding, soft clustering, and generative topographic reconstruction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] QuIDE: Mastering the Quantized Intelligence Trade-off via Active Optimization
item.description: QuIDE proposes a unified metric for evaluating quantized neural networks, finding optimal bit-widths vary by task: 4-bit for LLMs, 8-bit for complex CNNs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Steering Without Breaking: Mechanistically Informed Interventions for Discrete Diffusion Language Models
item.description: Researchers propose an adaptive scheduler for discrete diffusion language models that improves steering by timing interventions to match when attributes emerge.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Rotation-Preserving Supervised Fine-Tuning
item.description: RPSFT preserves pretrained weight rotations during fine-tuning to improve out-of-domain generalization while maintaining in-domain performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Vertex-Softmax: Tight Transformer Verification via Exact Softmax Optimization
item.description: Vertex-Softmax achieves tighter certified verification of transformers by optimizing softmax bounds at constraint box vertices.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Hierarchical Multi-Scale Graph Neural Networks: Scalable Heterophilous Learning with Oversmoothing and Oversquashing Mitigation
item.description: Spectral graph neural network framework HMH addresses heterophilous learning by mitigating oversmoothing and oversquashing with near-linear complexity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LEAP: Unlocking dLLM Parallelism via Lookahead Early-Convergence Token Detection
item.description: LEAP accelerates diffusion language model decoding by detecting early-converging tokens, reducing denoising steps by approximately 30%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] $\xi$-DPO: Direct Preference Optimization via Ratio Reward Margin
item.description: ξ-DPO proposes a ratio reward margin approach to simplify hyperparameter tuning in reference-free preference optimization compared to SimPO.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TMPO: Trajectory Matching Policy Optimization for Diverse and Efficient Diffusion Alignment
item.description: TMPO aligns diffusion models via trajectory-level reward distribution matching instead of scalar maximization, reducing reward hacking and mode collapse.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Structural Interpretations of Protein Language Model Representations via Differentiable Graph Partitioning
item.description: SoftBlobGIN framework enhances protein language model interpretability by projecting representations onto contact graphs and learning functional residue substructures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] AESOP: Adversarial Execution-path Selection to Overload Deep Learning Pipelines
item.description: AESOP framework demonstrates adversarial attacks exploiting ML pipeline routing achieve 2407x FLOP inflation, 20x more effective than single-model attacks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Seeing the Needle in the Haystack: Towards Weakly-Supervised Log Instance Anomaly Localization via Counterfactual Perturbation
item.description: LogMILP, a weakly-supervised framework, localizes anomalies in logs at instance level using only bag-level labels via prototype-guided modeling and counterfactual perturbation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SURGE: Surrogate Gradient Adaptation in Binary Neural Networks
item.description: SURGE proposes learnable gradient compensation framework for binary neural networks training, addressing gradient mismatch in binarization operations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Test-Time Personalization: A Diagnostic Framework and Probabilistic Fix for Scaling Failures
item.description: Researchers develop a framework for test-time personalization in LLMs that diagnoses reward model failures and proposes a probabilistic fix for improved scaling.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SkillGen: Verified Inference-Time Agent Skill Synthesis
item.description: SkillGen synthesizes reusable agent skills from successful and failed trajectories with empirical verification of performance impact.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Finite Volume-Informed Neural Network Framework for 2D Shallow Water Equations: Rugged Loss Landscapes and the Importance of Data Guidance
item.description: Data-guided finite-volume neural networks overcome training failures in physics-informed models for shallow water equations, achieving 22× error reduction with sparse measurements.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] DisagMoE: Computation-Communication overlapped MoE Training via Disaggregated AF-Pipe Parallelism
item.description: DisagMoE reduces MoE training communication bottlenecks by disaggregating attention and FFN layers across GPU groups, achieving up to 1.8x training speedup.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] RT-Transformer: The Transformer Block as a Spherical State Estimator
item.description: Transformer block components naturally derive from geometric state estimation with latent states modeled as directions on a hypersphere.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] When and How to Canonize: A Generalization Perspective
item.description: Framework bounds generalization error of canonization methods, proving Hilbert curve canonization provides polynomial versus exponential covering number growth for point clouds.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ACSAC: Adaptive Chunk Size Actor-Critic with Causal Transformer Q-Network
item.description: ACSAC proposes adaptive chunk size selection in reinforcement learning using a causal Transformer critic, achieving state-of-the-art performance on long-horizon sparse-reward manipulation tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Comparative Study of Federated Learning Aggregation Strategies under Homogeneous and Heterogeneous Data Distributions
item.description: Research compares federated learning aggregation strategies across homogeneous and heterogeneous data distributions, revealing distinct accuracy and efficiency trade-offs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LoopUS: Recasting Pretrained LLMs into Looped Latent Refinement Models
item.description: LoopUS post-training framework converts pretrained LLMs into looped architectures to improve test-time reasoning performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Backbone-Equated Diffusion OOD via Sparse Internal Snapshots
item.description: Canonical Feature Snapshots enables efficient out-of-distribution detection in diffusion models using minimal sparse internal activations without full denoising.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Simpson's Paradox in Behavioral Curves: How Aggregation Distorts Parametric Models of User Dynamics
item.description: Simpson's Paradox distorts aggregated behavioral curves by 3-5x compared to individual user peaks, driven by differential user attrition.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Efficient LLM Reasoning via Variational Posterior Guidance with Efficiency Awareness
item.description: VPG-EA framework improves LLM reasoning efficiency using variational inference to filter inefficient paths and transfer efficient patterns from posterior distribution to prior policy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Trust Region Inverse Reinforcement Learning: Explicit Dual Ascent using Local Policy Updates
item.description: TRIRL enables monotonic improvement in inverse reinforcement learning using local policy updates without fully solving RL problems each iteration.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Switching System Theory of Q-Learning with Linear Function Approximation
item.description: Switching-system framework using joint spectral radius analyzes convergence of Q-learning with linear function approximation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ASD-Bench: A Four-Axis Comprehensive Benchmark of AI Models for Autism Spectrum Disorder
item.description: ASD-Bench systematically evaluates multiple ML model types for autism screening across age cohorts, finding age-specific diagnostic patterns.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Enabling Performant and Flexible Model-Internal Observability for LLM Inference
item.description: DMI-Lib provides asynchronous monitoring of LLM internal states during inference with 0.4-6.8% offline and 6% online overhead.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Newton's Lantern: A Reinforcement Learning Framework for Finetuning AC Power Flow Warm Start Models
item.description: Newton's Lantern uses reinforcement learning to improve neural warm starts for AC power flow problems, achieving better convergence on loaded grid instances.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Sampling More, Getting Less: Calibration is the Diversity Bottleneck in LLMs
item.description: Research shows LLM diversity collapse from miscalibration: valid tokens underranked and probability mass overly concentrated on few valid continuations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ClinicalBench: Stress-Testing Assertion-Aware Retrieval for Cross-Admission Clinical QA on MIMIC-IV
item.description: ClinicalBench tests clinical QA retrieval on real EHR notes, finding negation, temporality, and attribution affect accuracy; assertion-aware methods gain 22 percentage points.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Decomposing Evolutionary Mixture-of-LoRA Architectures: The Routing Lever, the Lifecycle Penalty, and a Substrate-Conditional Boundary
item.description: Analysis of evolutionary mixture-of-LoRA systems reveals router mechanism provides measurable perplexity gains while lifecycle procedures degrade overall performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Bicameral Model: Bidirectional Hidden-State Coupling Between Parallel Language Models
item.description: Coupled language models communicate through hidden states instead of text, improving arithmetic and logic task performance significantly.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] How Does Differential Privacy Affect Social Bias in LLMs? A Systematic Evaluation
item.description: Differential privacy reduces social bias in LLMs for some tasks but not uniformly across different evaluation paradigms.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Instructions shape Production of Language, not Processing
item.description: Instructions predominantly shape language model output generation rather than input processing, showing stronger correlation with model behavior.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ReVision: Scaling Computer-Use Agents via Temporal Visual Redundancy Reduction
item.description: ReVision reduces visual tokens in computer-use agents by 46% through redundancy elimination, improving performance and enabling longer action histories.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] RETUYT-INCO at BEA 2026 Shared Task 2: Meta-prompting in Rubric-based Scoring for German
item.description: RETUYT-INCO employed meta-prompting, where LLMs generate custom scoring prompts from training examples, to evaluate German short answers in BEA 2026 shared task.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] HEBATRON: A Hebrew-Specialized Open-Weight Mixture-of-Experts Language Model
item.description: Hebatron is an open-weight Hebrew-specialized mixture-of-experts language model that outperforms comparable models while requiring only 3B active parameters.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ReAD: Reinforcement-Guided Capability Distillation for Large Language Models
item.description: ReAD is a reinforcement-guided framework for LLM compression that optimizes capability distillation by modeling interdependencies between model abilities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Predicting Psychological Well-Being from Spontaneous Speech using LLMs
item.description: Twelve LLMs achieved up to 0.8 correlation predicting psychological well-being from spontaneous speech using domain-informed prompts.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SOMA: Efficient Multi-turn LLM Serving via Small Language Model
item.description: SOMA reduces multi-turn LLM serving costs by using small surrogate models adapted to conversation-specific contexts via LoRA fine-tuning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Large Language Models for Causal Relations Extraction in Social Media: A Validation Framework for Disaster Intelligence
item.description: Researchers assess Large Language Models' ability to extract causal relations from informal disaster posts, finding both promise and risks for emergency decision-support.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] An Empirical Study of Automating Agent Evaluation
item.description: Study finds frontier coding assistants achieve 30% success in agent evaluation; proposes EvalAgent system automating the process with domain expertise.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Deep Reasoning in General Purpose Agents via Structured Meta-Cognition
item.description: Deep Reasoning enables LLM agents to dynamically construct task-specific reasoning scaffolds through meta-reasoning, outperforming fixed scaffolding approaches by 24.8% on complex tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Freeze Deep, Train Shallow: Interpretable Layer Allocation for Continued Pre-Training
item.description: Researchers propose LayerTracer to identify which LLM layers to freeze during continued pre-training, showing training shallow layers while freezing deep layers outperforms full fine-tuning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Agent-BRACE: Decoupling Beliefs from Actions in Long-Horizon Tasks via Verbalized State Uncertainty
item.description: Agent-BRACE decouples LLM agent beliefs from actions using verbalized uncertainty labels to track environment state efficiently in long-horizon partially observable tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] StoicLLM: Preference Optimization for Philosophical Alignment in Small Language Models
item.description: Small LLMs trained on 300 Stoic examples achieve inward virtue alignment but fail at outward cosmopolitan duties.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Robust Biomedical Publication Type and Study Design Classification with Knowledge-Guided Perturbations
item.description: Semantic perturbation framework and domain-adversarial training improve biomedical publication classification robustness without sacrificing in-domain accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Study on Hidden Layer Distillation for Large Language Model Pre-Training
item.description: Hidden Layer Distillation improves perplexity in LLM pre-training but doesn't consistently outperform standard knowledge distillation on downstream tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Checkup2Action: A Multimodal Clinical Check-up Report Dataset for Patient-Oriented Action Card Generation
item.description: Checkup2Action: 2,000-report multimodal dataset enabling generation of structured action cards for patient guidance with safety constraints.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Taming Extreme Tokens: Covariance-Aware GRPO with Gaussian-Kernel Advantage Reweighting
item.description: Researchers propose a covariance-weighted optimization method to improve GRPO's exploration-exploitation balance, enhancing reasoning performance and training stability.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Three Regimes of Context-Parametric Conflict: A Predictive Framework and Empirical Validation
item.description: A three-regime framework resolves contradictory findings about whether language models defer to context or rely on training knowledge.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] BitLM: Unlocking Multi-Token Language Generation with Bitwise Continuous Diffusion
item.description: BitLM enables parallel multi-token generation via bitwise diffusion while preserving causal attention, improving inference speed and training efficiency.
